scores.feedback: The site demonstrates solid fundamentals with a clean dark theme, strong color contrast (green accent), and responsive mobile adaptation. However, it falls short of reference-level polish due to sparse content hierarchy, minimal micro-interactions, and underdeveloped credibility signals compared to Bain/Deloitte. Top 3 fixes: (1) Add animated scroll transitions and hover states to match enterprise consulting sophistication; (2) Expand visible value proposition and case study snippets above the fold; (3) Enhance footer with detailed credentials, awards, and client logos to establish institutional gravitas.

The site demonstrates solid modern design with excellent color contrast (neon yellow CTA against dark navy) and clean typography that works well across devices. However, it falls short of premium consulting firm standards due to minimal animation/micro-interactions, sparse content density that feels incomplete, and weak differentiation from generic startup templates. Top 3 fixes: (1) Add subtle scroll animations and hover states to match Bain/Deloitte's polish; (2) Expand hero copy with concrete value proposition and differentiators beyond generic consulting language; (3) Populate the full page with case studies, team bios, or project showcase to establish credibility—currently appears under-developed.

The site has solid dark mode aesthetics with good color contrast (lime yellow CTA pops well), clean typography, and responsive layouts across devices. However, it feels more like a polished student project than institutional consulting credibility—the sparse content, minimal animations, and lack of case studies/social proof undermine authority positioning. Top 3 fixes: (1) Add client logos or portfolio case studies to establish credibility, (2) Introduce subtle micro-interactions on scroll/hover to increase sophistication, (3) Expand body copy with concrete value propositions rather than relying on aspirational headlines alone.

The site has strong visual direction with a sophisticated dark theme, neon accent color, and clean Korean typography that works well for a student org. However, it falls short of Bain/Deloitte caliber due to sparse content hierarchy, minimal interactive elements beyond the CTA button, and a hero-only approach that doesn't demonstrate consulting substance. Top 3 fixes: (1) Add consulting-focused content sections below the fold with case study snippets, team credentials, or service areas; (2) Introduce subtle micro-interactions (scroll reveals, hover states) to match enterprise polish; (3) Strengthen credibility signals—testimonials, partner logos, or achievement metrics rather than relying on aesthetic alone.

Strong foundation with clean dark theme, bold typography, and strategic lime-green accent that creates visual pop. The hero feels contemporary but lacks the sophisticated micro-interactions and content depth that distinguish Bain/Deloitte (empty full-page scroll, minimal context about capabilities). Top 3 fixes: (1) Add subtle parallax/scroll animations and hover states to match enterprise-grade polish, (2) Expand hero section or add below-fold content blocks showcasing case studies/expertise areas, (3) Increase information hierarchy clarity—subtext feels disconnected from main headline.
